search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae allergic contact dermatitis telehealth guideline PDF
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Medicine selection and doses were outside documentation scope.
Commercial product guidance is not authoritative.
Diagnosis and complication conclusions remain clinician responsibilities.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for suspected or clinician-diagnosed allergic contact dermatitis; irritant dermatitis and every blistering, infectious, drug-related, or systemic rash are not fully covered.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA telehealth history, morphology, exposure, red-flag, and clinician-led management documentation; diagnosis, testing, treatment, and referral remain clinician-led.
UAE_applicability: Verified for Dubai Health Authority telehealth services.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 allergic-contact-dermatitis PDF, due for revision 2029-02-21, was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA allergic-contact-dermatitis issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports contact-dermatitis terminology, itch, redness, burning, blistering, exposure, distribution, morphology, and clinician-reviewed history or photographs.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports contact-dermatitis terminology, itch, burning, erythema, scale, vesicles, blistering, edema, weeping, distribution, exposure, occupation, products, temporal course, atopy, and clinician-observed morphology.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports fever, respiratory distress, rapidly worsening or spreading painful eczema, infection concerns, and clinician-documented return fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports fever or systemic illness, respiratory distress, rapidly worsening painful eczema, recurrent or spreading infection, extensive involvement, pregnancy, and clinician-led return or referral documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-reviewed exposure history, photographs, and patch testing without recommending tests.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed exposure history, photographs, patch testing, KOH or culture, and selected tests when diagnosis is uncertain, without recommending testing automatically.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-documented trigger avoidance, skin care, emollient, and topical-treatment decisions without medicines or doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting trigger avoidance, skin-care or emollient discussion, and clinician-stated topical treatment without mapping medicine names, doses, frequency, duration, or autonomous advice.

unresolved_source_gaps: Mucosal involvement, facial swelling, fixed duration presets, fixed one-to-two-week review, and broad irritant-dermatitis equivalence remain unsupported.
The workflow must not infer allergy, infection, trigger, medicine need, or referral urgency.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
